steps.title: Spot the contrast between limitation and achievement
steps.content:
The second sentence establishes that the painting “doesn't technically represent a fourth dimension” (a limitation).

The third sentence then reveals what the painting actually does achieve: by depicting the race through “multiple, simultaneous perspectives,” Metzinger offers a glimpse of this other universe. The transition must acknowledge the limitation while pivoting to this contrasting accomplishment.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: “That said” is the concession-contrast transition—it acknowledges one point while moving to a different angle on it. The other three extend the idea, restate it, or exemplify it—none of which bridge ideas this way. B is correct.
